title: astragalus douglasii var. douglasii
intro: Astragalus douglasii var. douglasii, also known as Douglas' milkvetch, is a perennial plant species in the legume family that is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, as well as its ability to stimulate the production of white blood cells. The health benefits of Astragalus douglasii var. douglasii include reducing stress and fatigue, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds, flu, and other illnesses. Astragalus douglasii var. douglasii can be prepared as a decoction, tincture, or powder, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
